MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FERGUSON ENTERPRISES, LLC ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS FERGUSON FACILITIES RENOVATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

Defendant's (FERGUSON ENTERPRISES, LLC) motion to strike is denied.

The court liberally construes the complaint per Code of Civil Procedure section 452. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts showing malice, oppression, or fraud to support the claim for punitive damages. (Civil Code section 3294(a).)

"[M]alice does not require actual intent to harm. [Citation.] Conscious disregard for the safety of another may be sufficient where the defendant is aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his or her conduct and he or she willfully fails to avoid such consequences." (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299; see also College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 713 ["For plaintiffs attempting to prove malice by showing a 'conscious disregard' of their rights as opposed to an actual intent to harm, the Act imposed additional requirements of 'despicable' and 'willful' defense conduct."].)

Here, plaintiff's allegations that defendant's officers, directors, and managing agents knew of the dangers of the shower door and continued to distribute it support the request for exemplary damages. (Complaint (CGC-24-613280), pars. 17-18, 26-30, 59-68.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Defendants' motion to consolidate case numbers CGC 23-607181 and 24-613280 for all purposes is granted; the first-filed shall be the lead action. The actions involve personal injuries allegedly caused by breakage of glass shower doors manufactured by the same defendant and installed by the same defendant at the same hotel - accidents that occurred in the same few weeks. Thus, the actions involve common questions of law and fact. (CCP 1048.)

Order is signed.
